title: cirrus: setup WSL logging
body:
To debug some of the WSL issues we are seeing try to setup logging, the script parts are taken from the offical logging script[1] but modified to use a predictable dir/filename we can upload in cirrus and I also removed the interactive prompts.

[1] https://raw.githubusercontent.com/microsoft/WSL/master/diagnostics/collect-wsl-logs.ps1

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Luap99* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [Luap99] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

@baude or maybe @l0rd PTAL
Can either of you figure this out? I think cirrus wise my changes should be around what we need but I cannot for my life figure this powershell script stuff out and I have no windows box so I cannot tests anything basically and I really don't have the time to repush all at the time just to test something.

I have tried to split the script into two parts ([start collecting](https://gist.github.com/l0rd/9179aade0bd332554b3cc03a3f2ca040#file-win-collect-wsl-logs-start-ps1) and [stop collecting](https://gist.github.com/l0rd/9179aade0bd332554b3cc03a3f2ca040#file-win-collect-wsl-logs-stop-ps1)) and it worked fine locally.

We could try executing the first one [right before tests execution](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/contrib/cirrus/win-podman-machine-test.ps1#L44) and the second [in function `RunCommand` (where we execute logformatter)](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/contrib/cirrus/win-lib.ps1#L105) (making sure that the Cirrus job doesn't fail if the WSL log collection fails).
Thanks, I pushed the changes. Hopefully this works for CI.
A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.
I don't have time to play with this as I don't understand the windows stuff. If someone else want to debug the WSL start issues we CI please take this over.
